events.name: AI Builders Berlin
events.description:
We're sharing the latest and most practical tech for building AI agents. Topics you'll hear us talk about: Vibecoding, OpenClaw, Distills, Evals, LLMOps & anything about Agents.
🖥️ Innovative AI Demos
🎤 Builder Tool Demos - from EU or Silicon Valley leaders
🤝 🍻 A LOT of time... for informal networking
🍕 Yummy Pizza & Snacks
// FOR WHO?
✅ Devs, Product peeps, Data scientists, ML engineers, Founders, Hobby builders
✅ Anyone actively building
⚠️ Some technical knowledge highly recommended!*
// AGENDA
17:30 🤝 Walk-in
18:00 🤝 Networking
18:30 💬 Included Pizza (be early!)
18:45 🎤 TBA
19:00 💬 Networking Break
19:30 💻 TBA
19:45 💻 TBA
20:00 🤝 Networking & Free Drinks
21:00 🎯 End
// FAQ
• What's AI Builders? We're a community of 5000+ AI nerds. We're building a democratic AI platform, to make our community completely co-owned and co-governed by you (yes, all attendees).
• Will it be recorded? Yes, some AI Builders Berlin sessions are streamed live and recorded. By attending, you consent to being photographed and filmed. Let us know if you don't want to be on camera.
• I'm not technical. Can I come? Yes, but to actually enjoy the meetup, we recommend to understand basic LLMs lingo and concepts: Agents, Embeddings, RAG, MCPs, Inference, Structured Output, Finetuning, APIs, Reinforcement Learning.
• Why do I need to pay?
1). So we know how many people will show up, less food waste + better allocation of limited seats.
2). Sponsorships don’t cover all our costs (yet)!
• Can I get a free ticket? Can I volunteer? Money should never be the reason to not join. If you are in need of a sponsored ticket and/or want to help out by coming 1.5 hour early to help set up, message us here.
// Location
We look forward to hang out with you:CIC (Cambridge Innovation Campus)Right next to Görlitzer Park.Thanks to our friends at Æthos

// Vibes
events.descriptionSummary: Hands-on meetup diving into cutting-edge AI agent tools like Vibecoding and OpenClaw with live demos from leading builders, plus plenty of networking and pizza for devs, ML engineers, founders, and anyone actively shipping AI. Technical chops recommended to get the most out of it.

events.name: Encode Hackathon and Conference
events.description:
We’re excited to return to London for another year of hands-on creation, learning, and community building. Encode London 2026 brings together developers, founders, researchers, students, and innovators for a dual-track experience: a high-energy hackathon alongside a day of talks and discussions on the cutting edge of AI and Web3.
This year’s event continues our mission of fostering world-class builders while creating a space to explore new technologies, collaborate with peers, and bring ambitious ideas to life.

🛠️ Hackathon Overview
A full 3 days of non-stop hacking, where teams will build projects aligned with emerging AI and Web3 themes. Participants can expect challenges, bounties, mentorship, and the chance to showcase their work to a panel of judges.
Final details—including themes, prize categories, and challenge tracks—will be announced soon.

📚 Conference Day
On Saturday, the event transforms into a conference spotlighting the latest developments, trends, and thought leadership across AI and Web3.
Expect talks, insights, technical deep dives, and conversations with builders, researchers, and industry leaders. Speakers and sessions will be revealed closer to the event.

🎨 Event Themes
We’ll share the 2026 themes soon—including both technical and creative tracks designed to help builders explore emerging ideas in AI, crypto, and decentralised systems.

🗓 Event Timeline
A full weekend of hacking, learning, and community.
The exact schedule—including opening ceremony, workshops, conference sessions, judging, and finale—will be released ahead of the event.

🏆 Prizes, Challenges & Partners
Expect exciting bounties, partner challenges, and category awards.
All partners, prizes, and judges for Encode London 2026 will be announced in the coming months.

Venue: The Encode Hub
The event will once again take place at the Encode Hub—our community space for AI and Web3 builders in the heart of Shoreditch.


🤝 Partnership & Sponsorship
Interested in sponsoring, partnering, or getting involved?
Contact katerina@encode.club for more details.
events.descriptionSummary: Encode London 2026 will bring together developers, founders, researchers, and students for an intense weekend of AI and Web3 innovation, featuring a three-day hackathon with mentorship and prizes, followed by a conference filled with expert talks and technical deep dives from industry leaders.

This 5,000+ person community event will showcase the latest in LLMOps and agent building while you grab free drinks and connect with other developers actively shipping AI projects.

events.name: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - Relationships Under Founder Pressure
events.description:
Being a product founder, especially in the AI and tech space, comes with unique pressures: intense decision-making, constant context-switching, emotional isolation, and the invisible weight of carrying vision, team, and capital all at once.
October 13 Topic: Relationships Under Founder Pressure - “Your startup is growing. Is your marriage shrinking?”
Focus on:
Neglected partnerships: How long hours and constant mental load can quietly erode time and attention for partners.
Founder–family resentment: When loved ones feel like the startup always comes first.
Emotional unavailability: Being physically present but mentally stuck in founder mode.
Who it's for:
This meetup is a space for tech product founders to drop the pitch, remove the pressure, and connect on a human level. This event is not open to service providers.
Join New Tech Northwest Founder Brett Greene, PCC, executive coach and mental health advocate for founders, for a facilitated conversation focused on the emotional and psychological challenges of entrepreneurship. 

This is not a panel or a lecture, it's a confidential, founder-only circle designed to foster open dialogue, reflection, and peer connection. You're not alone in your founder challenges.
What to expect:
A safe, confidential environment for honest conversations
Facilitated group prompts focused on emotional resilience, identity, effectiveness, and overwhelm
Peer insight and shared experiences from others walking the same path
Opportunities to build friendships with fellow founders
Whether you're thriving, barely holding it together, or somewhere in between, you’re welcome here.
Agenda:
12:45pm - Check in and networking. Arrive early and allow time to find parking and walk to the venue.

1:00pm - Welcome guests and dive into today's topic. You'll be in a group of 3-4 founders to talk about the topic or a hot topic for you around founder life for 20-30 mins. Then you'll rotate into a new group of 3-4 people for another 20-30 mins.
Space is limited to maintain intimacy and depth.
🚗 Quick heads up: there is limited parking at the pier (daily rate).
Please use street parking or a nearby public lot.
📍 Getting There:
AI House is located about 400 feet out onto the pier from Alaskan Way. Walk all the way down and look for the white staircase above AQUA restaurant. Enter the building and head up to the 3rd floor.

RSVP required. This event is for tech product founders only to create a quality peer support experience. Thank you for understanding.
events.descriptionSummary: Tech product founders will explore the emotional toll of startup life on personal relationships in a confidential peer-led discussion facilitated by Brett Greene, an executive coach and mental health advocate. This intimate session offers a safe space to share struggles and gain support from fellow founders navigating similar pressures.
